| 1-2-1-platform-abstraction-headers | 3 | 2026-03-05 02:32 | 2026-03-05 15:43 | 13.18 h |

| 1-3-1-sdl3-dependency-integration | 3 | 2026-03-05 06:55 | 2026-03-05 15:53 | 8.97 h |
| 1-4-1-build-documentation | 2 | 2026-03-05 16:04 | 2026-03-05 17:03 | 1.00 h |

**Average Lead Time: 12.71 h (0.53 d)**
- Min: 1.00 h (1-4-1 — ran sequentially after predecessors)
- Max: 27.28 h (1-1-2 — required 7 retries and 4 regressions on code-review-qg)

### 4.3 Flow Efficiency

Active pipeline time vs. total lead time per story:

| Story | Active Time | Lead Time | Flow Efficiency |
|-------|-------------|-----------|----------------|
| 1-1-1-macos-cmake-toolchain | 4,980 s (1.38 h) | 14.38 h | 9.6% |
| 1-1-2-linux-cmake-toolchain | 3,960 s (1.10 h) | 27.28 h | 4.0% |
| 1-2-1-platform-abstraction-headers | 6,787 s (1.89 h) | 13.18 h | 14.3% |
| 1-2-2-platform-library-backends | 8,053 s (2.24 h) | 11.44 h | 19.6% |
| 1-3-1-sdl3-dependency-integration | 5,776 s (1.60 h) | 8.97 h | 17.9% |
| 1-4-1-build-documentation | 3,340 s (0.93 h) | 1.00 h | 93.2%* |

**Average Flow Efficiency: 26.4%**

> *1-4-1 ran in a single uninterrupted session (no wait time between steps), hence near-100% efficiency. Excluding 1-4-1, the average is 13.1% — consistent with industry baseline of 15-25%.

Industry benchmark: 15-25% is typical. Sprint 1 at 26.4% (or 13.1% adjusted) is at or just above baseline — acceptable for first sprint with pipeline learning.

### 4.4 Flow Distribution

All 6 stories are Enabler type (infrastructure/platform foundation). No Feature, Defect, or Debt stories in this sprint.

| Enabler | 6 | 18 | 100% |

This is expected — Sprint 1 is the Platform Foundation phase. Feature work begins in Sprint 2 (EPIC-2).

### 4.5 WIP Violations

Stories executed predominantly in sequence due to dependency chain. No simultaneous in-progress violations detected (0 WIP violations).

### 4.6 Pipeline Step Duration Summary

| Step | n | Avg Duration | Min | Max |
|------|---|-------------|-----|-----|
| create-story | 6 | 276 s | 203 s | 338 s |
| validate-story | 6 | 149 s | 125 s | 195 s |
| atdd | 6 | 259 s | 175 s | 307 s |
| dev-story | 6 | 1,077 s | 287 s | 2,398 s |
| completeness-gate | 6 | 132 s | 91 s | 195 s |
| code-review-analysis | 5 | 574 s | 290 s | 940 s |
| code-review-qg | 5 | 1,332 s | 1,039 s | 1,907 s |
| code-review-finalize | 5 | 568 s | 297 s | 1,005 s |

**Pipeline bottleneck (by duration): code-review-qg** — highest average duration (1,332 s) and most failures (13 failures on 1-1-2 alone). Optimizing this step will have the highest throughput impact in future sprints.

| 1-2-2 | MUPlatform Library with win32/posix Backends | 5 | Enabler | 11.44 h | 1 | 0 |

**Notable:** Story 1-1-2 (Linux toolchain) required the most rework — 7 retries and 4 regressions on `code-review-qg`. This reflects the Linux toolchain complexity and MinGW cross-compile edge cases. All were self-healed by the pipeline.

## Next Steps

1. Run `sprint-retrospective` for Sprint 1 to capture lessons learned (optional per sprint-status.yaml)
2. Run `sprint-planning` to define Sprint 2 — EPIC-2 stories (SDL3 windowing & input): 5 stories, 17 pts
3. Update sprint-status.yaml: sprint-1 status → `complete`
4. EPIC-1 completion gate check: all 6 stories done — verify epic-1 `status:` moves to `done`

**Key retrospective themes to explore:**
- 1-1-2 Linux toolchain: 7 retries on code-review-qg — what drove the failures? Improve quality before review step.
- 1-4-1 achieved 93% flow efficiency — sequential handoff pattern is highly efficient. Preserve this for future documentation stories.
- All enabler sprint: next sprints will mix Feature + Enabler. Monitor flow distribution for balance.
